get.description:
Performing a GET /api/actiondefinitions call will query for
available Actions defined in the system. This includes the data needed
to populate an Action in the system.

parameters.description: Filtering action definitions by the availability of being scheduled
parameters.name: scheduleEnabled

schema.description: Filtering action definitions by the availability of being scheduled

parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page
parameters.name: pageSize

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:action-definitions xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:pageInfo totalCount="2">
        <ops:page>0</ops:page>
        <ops:pageSize>10</ops:pageSize>
    </ops:pageInfo>
    <ops:links>
        <ops:link href="/suite-api/api/actiondefinitions?page=0&pageSize=10" rel="SELF" name="current">
            <ops:description>self</ops:description>
        </ops:link>
        <ops:link href="/suite-api/api/actiondefinitions?page=0&pageSize=10" rel="RELATED" name="first">
            <ops:description>first</ops:description>
        </ops:link>
        <ops:link href="/suite-api/api/actiondefinitions?page=0&pageSize=10" rel="RELATED" name="last">
            <ops:description>last</ops:description>
        </ops:link>
    </ops:links>
    <ops:action-definition id="VMWARE-Power Off VM" type="UPDATE" canRecommend="true">
        <ops:displayName>Power Off VM</ops:displayName>
        <ops:actionAdapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:actionAdapterKindKey>
        <ops:contextAdapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:contextAdapterKindKey>
        <ops:contextResourceKindKey>VirtualMachine</ops:contextResourceKindKey>
        <ops:contextIds>PowerOffVM</ops:contextIds>
        <ops:scheduleEnabled>true</ops:scheduleEnabled>
    </ops:action-definition>
    <ops:action-definition id="VMWARE-Power On VM" type="UPDATE" canRecommend="true">
        <ops:displayName>Power On VM</ops:displayName>
        <ops:actionAdapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:actionAdapterKindKey>
        <ops:contextAdapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:contextAdapterKindKey>
        <ops:contextResourceKindKey>VirtualMachine</ops:contextResourceKindKey>
        <ops:contextIds>PowerOnVM</ops:contextIds>
        <ops:scheduleEnabled>true</ops:scheduleEnabled>
    </ops:action-definition>
</ops:action-definitions>

200.description: List of ActionDefinitions in the system

get.summary: Look up Action Definitions in the system

post.description:
Performing a POST /api/actions/{id} will attempt to trigger the
action with specified id. It is considered a best practice to use the actionExecution field from the
POST /api/action/{id}/query that has been previously executed to populate
the body of the request. Please note that additional values may be required in order to make
the populateAction response body valid.

parameters.description: The id for the action to be executed (can be retrieved via: GET /api/actiondefinitions)
parameters.name: id

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:action-execution contextId="PowerOffVM" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:contextResourceId>7e780215-da07-4da1-9167-cd6892dcfdd8</ops:contextResourceId>
    <ops:parameterGroup resourceId="7e780215-da07-4da1-9167-cd6892dcfdd8"/>
</ops:action-execution>

requestBody.description: Describe instance data about performing an action.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:str-values xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:values>6fda92f5-0c4e-4ca0-a3c1-39121fd354a9</ops:values>
</ops:str-values>

200.description: List of Task Ids for the action run.

post.summary: Perform an Action on one or more resources

post.description:
Performing a POST /api/actions/{id}/query would yield a response object that
includes the resources that the action would apply to with the populated values of each action
context field for the resource. The response contains information about the parameters to the
action's method. It also includes a sample input that would be used for running the action with
POST /api/actions/{id} if you chose to run it on all applicable resources
and not change the default input values.

The json result inside actionExecution can be used directly as input perform the action. In the xml
result, the xml element ops:action-execution will need namespace binding added to it in order to use it
in the request.
xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/"

parameters.description: The Id for the action to be populate.
parameters.name: id

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:action-population xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:contextResourceId>c5af764a-d4d5-4258-829c-8acb0604e4c7</ops:contextResourceId>
</ops:action-population>

requestBody.description: The additional information needed to populate the action.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:populated-action id="VMWARE-Power Off VM" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:actionContext id="Power Off VM">
        <ops:fields>
            <ops:field id="Name" name="VM Name" component="textfield" input="false" displayOrder="0" hidden="false">
                <ops:expressionResults>
                    <ops:expressionResult>MyVMName</ops:expressionResult>
                </ops:expressionResults>
            </ops:field>
        </ops:fields>
    </ops:actionContext>
    <ops:methodParameters>
        <ops:methodParameter id="mOR" dataType="String" isRequired="true" isList="false"/>
    </ops:methodParameters>
    <ops:action-execution contextId="PowerOffVM">
        <ops:contextResourceId>7e780215-da07-4da1-9167-cd6892dcfdd8</ops:contextResourceId>
        <ops:parameterGroup resourceId="7e780215-da07-4da1-9167-cd6892dcfdd8"/>
    </ops:action-execution>
</ops:populated-action>

post.summary: Populate the information needed to run an action

get.summary: Get the meta-data for properties for a specific Resource Kind supported by a specific Adapter Kind

get.description:
Optionally filter these resources based on resource name. The resource name (specified as a query parameter) will be used for doing a partial match. However, if the resource identifiers and their values are specified, name is ignored and the API enforces all the mandatory (both uniquely identifying and required) identifiers are specified. This allows for looking up a single resource using a ResourceKey and allows the translation between a ResourceKey and a Resource UUID.

Example how to use the identifiers parameter (currently it cannot be used in our Swagger documentation):
/api/adapterkinds/{adapterKindKey}/resourcekinds/{resourceKindKey}/resources?identifiers[key1]=value1&identifiers[key2]=value2

parameters.description: The Adapter Kind Identifier
parameters.name: adapterKindKey

parameters.description: The Resource Kind Identifier
parameters.name: resourceKindKey

parameters.description: The Resource Name (partial match)
parameters.name: name

parameters.description: Key,value pairs representing the resource identifiers of a Resource
parameters.name: identifiers

parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page
parameters.name: pageSize

application/xml.example: No Custom Example is Available

patch.description:
This is typically used to indicate VMware Cloud Foundation operations that the certificates presented by
the (data source for which the adapter instance) was created are indeed valid
certificates.
Typically the response of the POST /api/adapters API in its entirety needs to be
sent as request body when making this PATCH /api/adapters API invocation.
NOTE: In case the provided certificate list is empty, the adapter instance self-signed certificates will be reset.
NOTE: for VCF and vCenter cloud accounts please use /api/integrations/vcenters and /api/integrations/vcf APIs respectively

        <ops:resourceKindKey>VMwareAdapter Instance</ops:resourceKindKey>
        <ops:resourceIdentifiers>
            <ops:resourceIdentifier>
                <ops:identifierType name="AUTODISCOVERY" dataType="STRING" isPartOfUniqueness="true"/>

            <ops:resourceIdentifier>
                <ops:identifierType name="PROCESSCHANGEEVENTS" dataType="STRING" isPartOfUniqueness="true"/>
                <ops:value>true</ops:value>
            </ops:resourceIdentifier>
            <ops:resourceIdentifier>
                <ops:identifierType name="VCURL" dataType="STRING" isPartOfUniqueness="true"/>

</ops:adapter-instance>

In case of VCenter adapter instance, UI/license extension registration should be run separately.
NOTE: for VCF and vCenter cloud accounts please use /api/integrations/vcenters and /api/integrations/vcf APIs respectively

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:alert-definitions xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:alert-definition>
        <ops:id>f080147f-c60d-4f08-8801-b602e2931b6f</ops:id>
        <ops:name>High CPU/Memory Usage</ops:name>
        <ops:description>CPU or MEM usage too High</ops:description>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:resourceKindKey>HostSystem</ops:resourceKindKey>
        <ops:waitCycles>3</ops:waitCycles>
        <ops:cancelCycles>2</ops:cancelCycles>
        <ops:type>0</ops:type>
        <ops:subType>0</ops:subType>
        <ops:states>
            <ops:state severity="WARNING">
                <ops:composite-symptom-set>
                    <ops:operator>AND</ops:operator>
                    <ops:symptom-sets>
                        <ops:symptom-set>
                            <ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>Symptom5</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>!Symptom6</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                            </ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                            <ops:alertConditions>
                                <ops:alertCondition>
                                    <ops:waitCycles>5</ops:waitCycles>
                                    <ops:cancelCycles>5</ops:cancelCycles>
                                    <ops:severity>CRITICAL</ops:severity>
                                    <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                                        <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
                                        <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                                        <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                                        <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                                        <ops:targetKey>cpu|availablemhz</ops:targetKey>
                                        <ops:thresholdType>STATKEY</ops:thresholdType>
                                    </ops:condition>
                                </ops:alertCondition>
                            </ops:alertConditions>
                            <ops:relation>DESCENDANT</ops:relation>
                            <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
                            <ops:resourceKindKey>Datastore</ops:resourceKindKey>
                            <ops:aggregation>ALL</ops:aggregation>
                            <ops:symptomSetOperator>AND</ops:symptomSetOperator>
                        </ops:symptom-set>
                        <ops:symptom-set>
                            <ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>Symptom100</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>Symptom101</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                            </ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                            <ops:relation>SELF</ops:relation>
                            <ops:aggregation>ALL</ops:aggregation>
                            <ops:symptomSetOperator>OR</ops:symptomSetOperator>
                        </ops:symptom-set>
                    </ops:symptom-sets>
                </ops:composite-symptom-set>
                <ops:impact>
                    <ops:impactType>BADGE</ops:impactType>
                    <ops:detail>health</ops:detail>
                </ops:impact>
            </ops:state>
        </ops:states>
        <ops:forVCDTenants>false</ops:forVCDTenants>
    </ops:alert-definition>
    <ops:alert-definition>
        <ops:id>AlertDefinition-147ff080-8801-c60d-4f08-931b6fb602e2</ops:id>
        <ops:name>MyAlertDefinition</ops:name>
        <ops:description>An Alert Definition associated with ESX host</ops:description>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:resourceKindKey>HostSystem</ops:resourceKindKey>
        <ops:waitCycles>3</ops:waitCycles>
        <ops:cancelCycles>2</ops:cancelCycles>
        <ops:type>0</ops:type>
        <ops:subType>0</ops:subType>
        <ops:states>
            <ops:state severity="CRITICAL">

post.description:
If the Notification plugin is a standard email plugin, then any untrusted certificates from the secure SMTP server
is returned as part of the response, the api client should invoke a PATCH call to store these certificates.
By default the notification plugin is in disabled state after being created.
Use the modifyAlertPluginState API to enable the plugin.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:notification-plugin xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:pluginTypeId>StandardEmailPlugin</ops:pluginTypeId>
    <ops:name>Notification Plugin Sample</ops:name>
    <ops:description>Where to notify Example</ops:description>
    <ops:configValues>
        <ops:configValue name="IS_SECURE_CONNECTION">true</ops:configValue>
        <ops:configValue name="SMTP_HOST">10.118.48.101</ops:configValue>
        <ops:configValue name="SMTP_PORT">25</ops:configValue>
        <ops:configValue name="senderEmailAddress">somebody@my.org</ops:configValue>
        <ops:configValue name="senderName">Somebody</ops:configValue>
    </ops:configValues>
</ops:notification-plugin>

put.description:
Before updating a notification plugin, the plugin should be disabled. During the update process
if there are any untrusted certificates returned by the server the patchAlertPlugin call should be made.
Once the update is successfully completed, use modifyAlertPluginState API to enable the plugin.

<ops:notification-plugin version="140592123" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

delete.description:
The field checkDependencies is added to preserve the initial behavior. By default it is set to false.

Case 1: When checkDependencies is false, the API has the following behavior:
Dependent Notification Rules are cascade deleted.
Dependent Report Schedules are unreferenced.
Dependent Tenant Email Configurations are always checked. If any then deletion is failed.

Case 2: When checkDependencies is true, the API has the following behavior:
All dependencies are checked. If any then deletion is failed.

To successfully delete the notification plugin, all conflicting dependencies must be removed.

parameters.description: The identifier of the plugin instance
parameters.name: pluginId

parameters.description: Flag to check plugin dependencies
parameters.name: checkDependencies

204.description: The notification plugin is deleted successfully

POST /api/alerts?action=suspend&minutes=1

POST /api/alerts?action=takeownership
POST /api/alerts?action=releaseownership
POST /api/alerts?action=assignownership

post.description:
Install app monitoring agent on specified resource.
vCenters mapping should be present for this API to work.

Correct configuration for vCenters mapping -
vCenter is mapped to individual CP or CP part of non HA collector group.
vCenter is mapped to HA enabled collector group.

vCenters mapping APIs -
Create vCenters mappings API POST /api/applications/vccpmappings.
Delete vCenters mapping API DELETE /api/applications/vccpmappings.

post.description:
The auth source can be SSO/VIDM/LDAP/AD.
For the LDAP/AD auth source, the request should contain the name of the group.
For the SSO/VIDM auth source, request should contain the name of the group and the SSO/VIDM domain.
User-group's name can contain wildcards. Maximum of 25 user-groups are returned for SSO auth source.

parameters.description: The id of the auth source
parameters.name: id

delete.description:
During the deletion of the SSO SAML/VCF SSO auth source, the same 'username' and 'password' which has been used while the creating this sso auth source, should be sent to the payload object.
Note: Force flag is applicable only for VCF SSO auth source. If force is true, VMware Cloud Foundation Operations continues to attempt to delete the VCF SSO auth source even when errors occur.
If you set it to false, VMware Cloud Foundation Operations stops attempting to removethe auth source if VCF SSO server fails

parameters.description: Id of the auth source
parameters.name: sourceId

parameters.description: This flag is applicable only to VCF SSO authentication sources.
parameters.name: force

get.description:
The auth source can be SSO/LDAP/AD.
For the LDAP/AD auth source, the request should contain the name of the user.
For the SSO auth source, request should contain the name of the user and the sso domain.
User-group's username can contain wildcards. Maximum of 25 user-groups are returned for SSO auth source.

post.description:
Performing a POST /api/auth/token/acquire would yield a response object that includes token and its validity.
HTTP Status 401 will be sent back if the authentication operation has failed.
NOTE: The validity of the acquired token is extended after each call and is set to 6 hours from the last call.

post.description:
If the authSourceId is specified(in the request), then the user group will be imported from the corresponding auth source (LDAP/AD/SSO/VIDM/VIDB).Note that the id of the user group has to be null.
For LDAP/AD groups the distinguishedName should be provided in the name field.
The value of displayName is used only while importing LDAP/AD groups, and if it is not provided then the value of name will be assigned to it. For SSO/VIDM groups the value of name is assigned to displayName.
NOTE: Before importing please make sure that the group exists in the specified authSource by using the following API - /api/auth/sources/{id}/usergroups/search . Otherwise, if you try to import a non-existing group, a new one will be created with the specified authSourceId.
NOTE: In the case of vIDB user group, the import occurs based on the provided externalId. If other provided details are incorrect, they will be asynchronously updated with the correct values right after the import operation is completed.

    </ops:userIds>

        <ops:role-permission allowAllObjects="true">

                    <ops:resourceKind>resource_kind</ops:resourceKind>
                    <ops:name>traversal_spec_name</ops:name>
                </ops:traversal-spec-instance>
            </ops:traversal-spec-instances>
        </ops:role-permission>
    </ops:role-permissions>
</ops:user-group>

Either an abbreviation such as "GMT", "PDT" can be specified,

formats and representations are not supported.

get.description: Get details of the specified configuration file.

parameters.description:
Configuration file path in {configFileType}/{fileName} format.
Allowed values for configFileType: TxtWidgetConfig (Text Widget Content), ReskndMetricConfig (Widget Metric Configuration), TopoWidgetConfig (Topology Widget Configuration) , SolutionConfig (Management Packs Configuration).
Note: any sub folders, visible via UI, should be skipped in the path.
parameters.name: path

400.description: Request parameter is invalid

get.summary: Get the last import operation details

post.description:
The "Content-Type" of the request is multipart/form-data. If the force option is set to true, content will be overwritten. By default the flag is true.
NOTE: If the content file contains integration accounts, then upon completion of the import process, it is recommended to check them. Some collectors or collector groups may have become unbound from the accounts they serve and should be reconnected. Additionally, you will need to re-accept all untrusted certificates manually. Finally ensure that all your adapter instances are started as some of these may not have been restarted automatically.

parameters.description: Key for decrypting sensitive information
parameters.name: EncryptionPassword

parameters.description: True to force solution content overwrite
parameters.name: force

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:content-import id="bb1f4c64-b1e9-4a3f-a051-790d926342d2" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:fileName>contentFile</ops:fileName>
    <ops:force>false</ops:force>
    <ops:links>
        <ops:link href="/suite-api/api/content/operations/import" rel="RELATED" name="ImportStatusCheckURL">
            <ops:description>Status of the last import operation</ops:description>
        </ops:link>
    </ops:links>
</ops:content-import>

get.description: Gets all the Credential Kinds defined in the system.  Optionally filter by adapter kind keys.

parameters.description: Filter by Adapter Kind Key
parameters.name: adapterKind

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:credential-kinds xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:credential-kind id="PRINCIPALCREDENTIAL">
        <ops:name>Principal Credential</ops:name>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:fields>
            <ops:field key="USER" valueType="STRING" required="false" enumerated="false"/>
            <ops:field key="PASSWORD" valueType="STRING" required="false" enumerated="false"/>
        </ops:fields>
    </ops:credential-kind>
    <ops:credential-kind id="PRINCIPALCREDENTIAL">
        <ops:name>Principal Credential</ops:name>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>InvSvcAdapter</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:fields>
            <ops:field key="VCADMIN" valueType="STRING" required="false" enumerated="false"/>
            <ops:field key="VCADMINPASSWORD" valueType="STRING" required="false" enumerated="false"/>
        </ops:fields>
    </ops:credential-kind>
    <ops:credential-kind id="HTTP">
        <ops:name>Http Credential</ops:name>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>ExampleAdapter</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:fields>
            <ops:field key="UserType" defaultValue="User" valueType="string" required="false" enumerated="true">
                <ops:enumeratedValues>
                    <ops:enumeratedValue>Admin</ops:enumeratedValue>
                    <ops:enumeratedValue>User</ops:enumeratedValue>
                    <ops:enumeratedValue>Anonymous</ops:enumeratedValue>
                </ops:enumeratedValues>
            </ops:field>
            <ops:field key="Password" valueType="STRING" required="false" enumerated="false"/>
        </ops:fields>
    </ops:credential-kind>
</ops:credential-kinds>

get.summary: Get all global setting values

get.description:
Find all the name-key mappings here:

Action History - ACTION_RETENTION_IN_DAYS,
Deleted Objects - DELETED_OBJECTS_RETENTION_IN_HOURS,
Deletion Scheduling Interval - DELETION_SCHEDULING_INTERVAL_IN_HOURS,
Object History - OBJECT_HISTORY_RETENTION_IN_DAYS,
Generated Reports Retention - GENERATED_REPORTS_RETENTION_TIME_IN_MONTHS,
Session Timeout - SESSION_TIMEOUT_IN_MINUTES,
Symptoms/Alerts - CANCELLED_ALERTS_SYMPTOMS_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS,
Time Series Data Retention - TIME_SERIES_DATA_RETENTION_IN_MONTHS,
Additional Time Series Retention - ROLLUP_TIME_SERIES_DATA_RETENTION_IN_MONTHS,
Deleted Users - DELETED_USERS_RETENTION_IN_DAYS,
External Event Based Active Symptoms - OUTDATED_EVENT_BASED_SYMPTOMS_CANCELING_ENABLED,
External Event Based Active Symptoms - ACTIVE_EVENT_BASED_SYMPTOMS_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS,
Dynamic Threshold Calculation - DYNAMIC_THRESHOLDING_UPDATE_TIME_IN_MINUTES,
Cost Calculation - COST_CALCULATION_TIME_IN_MINUTES,
Deprecated from VMware Aria Operations 8.14: Customer Experience Improvement Program - TELEMETRY_ENABLED,
Allow vCenter users to log into VMware Cloud Foundation Operations from vCenter clients - VC_USER_LOGIN_FROM_VC_WEBUI_ALLOWED,
System access URL - BASE_URL,
Automated Actions - AUTOMATED_ACTIONS_ENABLED,
Enable Standard Certification Validation - ENABLE_CERTIFICATE_VALIDATION_STANDARD_WAY,
Concurrent UI login sessions - ALLOW_CONCURRENT_LOGIN_SESSIONS,
Allow non-imported vIDM user access - ALLOW_NON_IMPORTED_VIDM_USERS_ACCESS,
Dynamic Threshold Calculation - DYNAMIC_THRESHOLDING_ENABLED,
Cost Calculation - COST_CALCULATION_ENABLED,
Deleted Objects - DELETED_OBJECTS_RETENTION_IN_HOURS_EXPANSION,
Generated Reports Retention - GENERATED_REPORTS_RETENTION_ENABLED,
Docker image default registry URL - DOCKER_IMAGE_REGISTRY_URL,
Tag Based Pricing Metrics - PRICING_TAG_DETAILED_METRICS,
Tag based Costing Metrics - COSTING_TAG_DETAILED_METRICS,
Cluster Utilization Ceiling Factor - CLUSTER_UTILIZATION_ROUND_OFF,
Generated Reports Retention - GENERATED_REPORTS_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS,
Threshold For Adapters Certificate Expiration Alert - CERTIFICATE_ALARM_DAYS,
Show Cost Savings - SHOW_COST_SAVINGS_RECLAMATION_SETTING,
Include Powered off VMs for Reclamation (flag) - RECLAIM_POWERED_OFF_VMS_ENABLED,
Include Powered off VMs for Reclamation (days) - RECLAIM_POWERED_OFF_VMS_DAYS,
Include Idle VMs for Reclamation - RECLAIM_IDLE_VMS_ENABLED,
Minimum number of days that VM has been idle for - RECLAIM_IDLE_VMS_DAYS,
Exclude VM provisioned in last - EXCLUDE_IDLE_VMS_PROVISIONED_IN_LAST_DAYS,
VM Idleness Criteria (MHz) - IDLENESS_INDICATOR_THRESHOLD_MHZ,
VM Idleness Criteria (%) - IDLENESS_INDICATOR_THRESHOLD_PERCENT,
Include Snapshots for Reclamation (flag) - RECLAIM_SNAPSHOTS_ENABLED,
Include Snapshots for Reclamation (days) - RECLAIM_SNAPSHOTS_DAYS,
Include Orphaned Disks for Reclamation (flag) - RECLAIM_ORPHANED_DISKS_ENABLED,
Include Orphaned Disks for Reclamation (days) - RECLAIM_ORPHANED_DISKS_DAYS,
Orphaned Disks Collection Time - ORPHANED_VMDK_COLLECTION_TIME_IN_MINUTES,
Generated Tenant Reports Retention (flag) - GENERATED_TENANT_REPORTS_RETENTION_ENABLED,
Generated Tenant Reports Retention (days) - GENERATED_TENANT_REPORTS_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS,
Credential Ownership Enforcement - ACTIVATE_CREDENTIAL_OWNERSHIP.

get.description:
The information returned includes the Health of the Service, uptime, when the service was started and etc.
Specifically the Health of the Service is 'OK', when the Service is running and responsive, otherwise it is 'Error'.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:services xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:service name="ADMINUI" health="OK"/>
    <ops:service name="API" health="OK"/>
</ops:services>

503.description: Not all services are not Healthy

get.summary: Get the health of all Services

get.description:
The information returned includes the Health of the Service, uptime, start time of the service and etc.
Specifically the Health of the Service is 'OK', when the Service is running and responsive, otherwise it is 'Error'.

if the adapter kind specified does already exist, then it must be of OPENAPI adapter kind type.
Also the API sanitizes the Push Adapter Kind key by removing invalid characters (e.g.: Embedded HTML & JS)

parameters.name: adapterKind

get.description: Retrieves a list of all VCF components (vCenter) that are eligible for custom role provisioning. Use this API to discover available components in your environment where you can create and apply custom roles for fine-grained access control.

200.description: List of eligible components retrieved successfully

get.summary: Get Eligible Components for Custom Roles

delete.description: Delete Iam Component Auth Source by either VCF Component ID or Component Type. Exactly one parameter (componentId or componentType) must be provided.

parameters.description: VCF Component ID. Either componentId or componentType must be provided, but not both. Note: Managed Component Auth sources for VCENTER and NSX_MANAGER components can be deleted using componentId.
parameters.name: componentId

parameters.description: VCF Management Component Type (enum). Either componentId or componentType must be provided, but not both. Only management components (VCF_OPERATIONS, VCF_AUTOMATION) are supported. For VCENTER and NSX_MANAGER, use componentId instead.
parameters.name: componentType

schema.description: VCF Management Component type

204.description: Iam Component Auth Source Deleted Successfully

delete.summary: Delete Iam Component Auth Source

post.description: Creates Iam Component Auth Source. Can be based on a VCF Component or a Management Component type.

requestBody.description: IAM Component Auth Source request payload

post.summary: Create Iam Component Auth Source (VCF or Management Component based).

post.description: Fetch Iam Component Auth Source. If no filterType and filterValue provided, returns all. If provided, fetch by filterType (VCF_COMPONENT, MANAGEMENT_COMPONENT, or VCF_INSTANCE) and corresponding filterValue. filterValue must be a valid UUID for VCF_COMPONENT and VCF_INSTANCE, or a valid ComponentType enum value for MANAGEMENT_COMPONENT.

requestBody.description: Query filter for Iam Component Auth Sources. If no filterType and filterValue provided, returns all. If provided, filterValue must be a valid UUID for VCF_COMPONENT and VCF_INSTANCE, or a valid ComponentType enum value for MANAGEMENT_COMPONENT.

In such cases response will be returned with http 200 status code, however the failed resources id's will be reported in the response body. Please check the status field of the response after executing this API, in order to make sure that the operation has fully succeeded.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:log-configuration xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

    <ops:settings>
        <ops:setting>
            <ops:name>com.vmware.info.logger.name</ops:name>

        </ops:setting>
        <ops:setting>
            <ops:name>com.vmware.debug.logger.name</ops:name>
            <ops:level>DEBUG</ops:level>
        </ops:setting>
    </ops:settings>
</ops:log-configuration>

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:maintenance-schedules xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:maintenance-schedule id="f5534f68-0139-4c57-aac0-fef0005d13d3" key="daily-ms1">
        <ops:schedule>
            <ops:hour>3</ops:hour>
            <ops:minuteOfTheHour>0</ops:minuteOfTheHour>
            <ops:duration>180</ops:duration>
            <ops:scheduleType>DAILY</ops:scheduleType>
            <ops:recurrence>1</ops:recurrence>
            <ops:expirationDate>01/20/2050</ops:expirationDate>
            <ops:timeZone>Asia/Yerevan</ops:timeZone>
        </ops:schedule>
    </ops:maintenance-schedule>
    <ops:maintenance-schedule id="0f5d8313-079c-4c00-ae9e-cb14d5e38443" key="monthly-ms1">
        <ops:schedule>
            <ops:hour>3</ops:hour>
            <ops:minuteOfTheHour>0</ops:minuteOfTheHour>
            <ops:duration>3</ops:duration>
            <ops:scheduleType>MONTHLY</ops:scheduleType>
            <ops:daysOfTheMonth>1 2</ops:daysOfTheMonth>
            <ops:months>1</ops:months>
            <ops:timeZone>Asia/Yerevan</ops:timeZone>
            <ops:expireRuns>3</ops:expireRuns>
        </ops:schedule>
    </ops:maintenance-schedule>
    <ops:maintenance-schedule id="d07b52b5-6839-4ced-8809-7e8afc527059" key="weekly-ms1">
        <ops:schedule>
            <ops:hour>15</ops:hour>
            <ops:minuteOfTheHour>3</ops:minuteOfTheHour>
            <ops:duration>1</ops:duration>
            <ops:scheduleType>WEEKLY</ops:scheduleType>
            <ops:recurrence>5</ops:recurrence>
            <ops:daysOfTheWeek>SATURDAY SUNDAY</ops:daysOfTheWeek>
            <ops:timeZone>Asia/Yerevan</ops:timeZone>
            <ops:expireRuns>7</ops:expireRuns>
        </ops:schedule>
    </ops:maintenance-schedule>
</ops:maintenance-schedules>

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:notification-templates xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:notificationTemplates>
        <ops:notificationTemplate templateId="a8107b20-0a38-4d7c-8292-dae0e5ca9f49" templateType="ALERT">
            <ops:pluginTypeId>WebhookPlugin</ops:pluginTypeId>
            <ops:name>Template</ops:name>
            <ops:description>WebhookPlugin Description</ops:description>
            <ops:contentConfigs>
                <ops:contentConfig>
                    <ops:baseResourceKindKeyStats>
                        <ops:resourceKindKey>
                            <ops:resourceKind>Resource kind</ops:resourceKind>
                            <ops:adapterKind>Adapter kind</ops:adapterKind>
                        </ops:resourceKindKey>
                        <ops:stats>
                            <ops:stat>Property key</ops:stat>
                            <ops:stat>Stat key</ops:stat>
                        </ops:stats>
                    </ops:baseResourceKindKeyStats>
                    <ops:ancestorResourceKindKeys>
                        <ops:ancestorResourceKindKey>
                            <ops:resourceKindKey>
                                <ops:resourceKind>Ancestor Resource kind</ops:resourceKind>
                                <ops:adapterKind>Ancestor Adapter kind</ops:adapterKind>
                            </ops:resourceKindKey>
                            <ops:stats>
                                <ops:stat>Ancestor Stat Key</ops:stat>
                                <ops:stat>Ancestor Property Key</ops:stat>
                            </ops:stats>
                        </ops:ancestorResourceKindKey>
                    </ops:ancestorResourceKindKeys>
                    <ops:descendantResourceKindKeys>
                        <ops:descendantResourceKindKey>
                            <ops:resourceKindKey>
                                <ops:resourceKind>Descendant Resource kind</ops:resourceKind>
                                <ops:adapterKind>Descendant Adapter kind</ops:adapterKind>
                            </ops:resourceKindKey>
                            <ops:stats>
                                <ops:stat>Descendant Property Key</ops:stat>
                                <ops:stat>Descendant Stat Key</ops:stat>
                            </ops:stats>
                        </ops:descendantResourceKindKey>
                    </ops:descendantResourceKindKeys>
                </ops:contentConfig>
            </ops:contentConfigs>
            <ops:formattingTemplate xsi:type="ops:webhookTemplate">
                <ops:newAlertTemplate>
                    <ops:endpoint>/endpoint</ops:endpoint>
                    <ops:headers>

This Timezone value will be typically used for Report Generation.
Either an abbreviation such as "GMT", "PDT" can be specified,
or a full name such as "America/Los_Angeles". Other
formats and representations are not supported.

    and an error message indicating the set of missing Resource Identifiers.

put.summary: Update a Resource in the system

post.description:
This adapter kind (referred to as 'Push' adapter kind) will be created if it does not
exist in the system and an instance of it will be associated with the Resource being created.
This adapter kind if it already exists must of OPENAPI adapter kind type.
The API will also create the missing Adapter Kind and Resource Kind
contained within the ResourceKey of the Resource if they do not exist.


Additional implementation notes:


  • When creating a Resource, if the Resource Identifiers that are unique and required
    are not specified, the API would return an error with HTTP status code of 500
    and an error message indicating the set of missing Resource Identifiers.

  • When creating a Resource, if the Resource Identifiers that are unique but not required
    are not specified, the Resource is created where the uniquely identifying Resource Identifiers
    that were not specified will have their value as an empty string.

parameters.name: adapterKindKey

    <ops:description>Description of this virtual machine</ops:description>
    <ops:resourceKey>
        <ops:name>VM1</ops:name>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:resourceKindKey>VirtualMachine</ops:resourceKindKey>
        <ops:resourceIdentifiers>
            <ops:resourceIdentifier>
                <ops:identifierType name="VMEntityObjectID" dataType="STRING" isPartOfUniqueness="true"/>
                <ops:value>vm-123</ops:value>
            </ops:resourceIdentifier>
            <ops:resourceIdentifier>
                <ops:identifierType name="VMEntityVCID" dataType="STRING" isPartOfUniqueness="true"/>
                <ops:value>CBAD174A-622E-4650-805A-064F824FFA76</ops:value>
            </ops:resourceIdentifier>
            <ops:resourceIdentifier>
                <ops:identifierType name="VMEntityName" dataType="STRING" isPartOfUniqueness="true"/>
                <ops:value>VM1</ops:value>

        </ops:resourceIdentifiers>

    <ops:credentialInstanceId>51f8f60d-4a39-4839-9924-698d022e2e10</ops:credentialInstanceId>

    <ops:relatedResources/>
</ops:resource>

delete.description:
Delete custom datacenters.
NOTE: Deletion of custom datacenters is not synchronous. As a result,
the delete operation may not happen immediately. Furthermore, there is no way
to track the status of the deletion operation. It is recommended to query back
the system and ensure that the resources (datacenters) with given ids are not present any more.
NOTE: This API is only for custom datacenters deletion. Any invalid or none custom datacenter ids in the request body will be ignored.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>

</ops:uuid-values>

delete.description: Bring the specified set of Resources out of Maintenance manually

parameters.description: A collection of Resource identifiers
parameters.name: id

200.description: The resource is brought out from maintenance successfully

delete.summary: Bring the specified set of Resources out of Maintenance manually

put.description:
The Resources can end up in two maintenance states - MAINTAINED OR MAINTAINED_MANUAL -
depending upon the inputs specified.


  • If duration/end time is specified, the Resources will be placed in MAINTAINED
    state and after the duration/end time expires, the state of the Resources would be automatically
    set to the state they were in before entering the maintenance window.
  • If duration/end time is not specified, the Resources will be placed in MAINTAINED_MANUAL state.
    Callers have to execute DELETE /suite-api/api/resources/maintained?id=ID1&id=ID2 API
    to set the Resources back to whatever state they were in.
  • If both duration and end time are specified, end time takes preference over duration.

parameters.description: The Resource identifiers
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The Duration for which this resource will be in maintenance (In Minutes)
parameters.name: duration

parameters.description: The End time of the maintenance window as a long value (Epoch time)
parameters.name: end

200.description: The resource is put in maintenance successfully

put.summary: Put all the specified Resource in Maintenance

put.description:
The query parameter id is for specifying the set of Resources that should
be monitored.
The optional query parameter adapterId determines which of the
Adapters need to be informed to start monitoring the Resources. If this is
not specified, then all of the Adapters will start monitoring the specified Resources.

parameters.description: The Resource identifiers that need to be monitored
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Collection of adapter instance identifiers that should start monitoring the Resources
parameters.name: adapterId

200.description: The resource monitoring is started successfully

put.description:
The query parameter id is for specifying the set of Resources that should
no longer be monitored.
The optional query parameter adapterId determines which of the
Adapters need to be informed to stop monitoring the Resources. If this is
not specified, then all of the Adapters will stop monitoring the specified Resources.

parameters.description: The Resource identifiers that no longer need to be monitored

parameters.name: adapterId

get.summary: Get all the available stat keys and their details for the specified resource

get.description:
This is a GET endpoint and the query parameters control the data that is retrieved.
If the number of Resources for which one needs to query data is large, it is recommended to
use the POST /api/stats/query endpoint to avoid the URL length limitations.
Takes the form:
api/resources/stats?resourceId=UUID1 OR
api/resources/stats?resourceId=UUID2&statKey=cpu|usagemhz&rollUpType=&begin=&end=

parameters.description: Array of Resource ids for which the stats should be fetched
parameters.name: resourceId

parameters.description: Array of stat keys for which the stats should be returned
parameters.name: statKey

parameters.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used
parameters.name: begin

schema.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

parameters.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used
parameters.name: end

schema.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used

parameters.description: Rollup Type requested by the user (e.g.: AVG, MIN, MAX etc.)
parameters.name: rollUpType

schema.description: Represents the type of Rollup required for the stats data

parameters.description: Interval type requested by the user (e.g: Days, Weeks, Months etc.)
parameters.name: intervalType

schema.description: The Interval types

parameters.description: Number for the interval type
parameters.name: intervalQuantifier

schema.description: Number for the interval type

parameters.description:
Indicates that the requested stats are based on some dynamic threshold.
Defaults to false which indicates that stats are not based on dynamic threshold.
parameters.name: dt

schema.description:
Indicates that the requested stats are based on some dynamic threshold.
Defaults to false which indicates that stats are not based on dynamic threshold.

parameters.description:
The maximum number of samples to return when querying latest.
When processing the get latest stats query if it is omitted a value of 1 is assumed.
A query for the latest samples is not compatible with specifying the RollUpType value.
parameters.name: latestMaxSamples

schema.description:
The maximum number of samples to return when querying latest.
When processing the get latest stats query if it is omitted a value of 1 is assumed.
A query for the latest samples is not compatible with specifying the RollUpType value.

parameters.description: Indicates whether specified statKey-s of query should be forcibly treated as metrics (does not include instanced stats)
parameters.name: metrics

schema.description: Indicates whether specified statKey-s of query should be forcibly treated as metrics (does not include instanced stats)

parameters.description:
Indicates whether to report only "current" stat values, i.e. skip the stat-s that haven't published any value
during recent collection cycles.
parameters.name: currentOnly

schema.description:
Indicates whether to report only "current" stat values, i.e. skip the stat-s that haven't published any value
during recent collection cycles.

parameters.description:
Indicates whether stat values should be wrapped for application/xml response content type.
This flag is false by default and not needed for application/json response.
If set to true "statValues" will be returned instead of "values" for all content types.
parameters.name: wrapStatValues

schema.description:
Indicates whether stat values should be wrapped for application/xml response content type.
This flag is false by default and not needed for application/json response.
If set to true "statValues" will be returned instead of "values" for all content types.

application/xml.example: No Custom Example is Available

get.description: Get the Top-N stats data for one or more Resources and a set of StatKeys

parameters.description:
The number of results. Keep in mind if a grouping contains multiple items (eg:
grouped by resource, with 4 metrics for the grouping resource) then returning the group with
4 metrics counts as 4 topN entries. If there are not enough remaining open topN entries to
return an entire group within the topN then the entire group is not returned. *
parameters.name: topN

schema.description:
The number of results. Keep in mind if a grouping contains multiple items (eg:
grouped by resource, with 4 metrics for the grouping resource) then returning the group with
4 metrics counts as 4 topN entries. If there are not enough remaining open topN entries to
return an entire group within the topN then the entire group is not returned. *

parameters.description: The type of grouping, if not set defaults to ResourceStatGroupBy.RESOURCE
parameters.name: groupBy

parameters.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used
parameters.name: begin

schema.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

parameters.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used
parameters.name: end

schema.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used

delete.description: Bring the Resource out of Maintenance manually

parameters.description: The Resource identifier
parameters.name: id

200.description: The resource is brought out from maintenance successfully

delete.summary: Bring the Resource out of Maintenance manually

put.description:
The Resource can end up in two maintenance states - MAINTAINED OR MAINTAINED_MANUAL -
depending upon the inputs specified.

  • If duration/end time is specified, the resource will be placed in MAINTAINED
    state and after the duration/end time expires, the resource state is automatically set to the state
    it was in before entering the maintenance window.
  • If duration/end time is not specified, the resource will be placed in MAINTAINED_MANUAL state.
    Callers have to execute DELETE /suite-api/api/resources/{id}/maintained API to set the Resource
    back to whatever state it was in.
  • If both duration and end time are specified, end time takes preference over duration.

parameters.description: The Resource identifier
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Duration for which this resource will be in maintenance (In Minutes)
parameters.name: duration

parameters.description: End time of the maintenance window as a long value (Epoch time)
parameters.name: end

200.description: The resource is put in maintenance successfully

get.summary: Get the related resources of a particular Relationship Type for a Resource

post.description:
• If at least a few of the Resource that are part of relatedIds list
    are valid resources then the operation is performed.

  • If there are few Resource that are part of relatedIds list
    that will result in a cyclical relationship, then those resources will be skipped.

NOTE: Adding relationship is not synchronous. As a
result, the add operation may not happen immediately. It is recommended
to query the relationships of the specific Resource back to ensure that the
operation was indeed successful.

parameters.description: The UUID of the source resource
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The type of relationship of the objects; Uses the RelationshipType.representation value
parameters.name: relationshipType

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:uuid-values xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:uuids>df6de846-0e97-408d-bcff-be0026eba68a b3a3b7ef-7871-4238-86f4-3a5f10294450</ops:uuids>
</ops:uuid-values>

requestBody.description: UUID of resources to add as related to this resource

204.description: The relationship is added successfully

404.description: All of the Resource that are part of the relatedIds list are invalid/non-existent

post.summary: Add relationships of given type to the resource with specified resourceId

put.description:
This API exposes replace semantics. Therefore, all the existing relationships of the
specified relationshipType will be removed and replaced with the resources
specified as part of the request body.

  • If at least a few of the Resource that are part of relatedIds list
    are valid resources then the operation is performed.

  • If there are few Resource that are part of relatedIds list
    that will result in a cyclical relationship, then the operation is aborted.

NOTE: Setting relationship is not synchronous.
As a result, the set operation may not happen immediately. It is recommended
to query the relationships of the specific Resource back to ensure that the
operation was indeed successful.

parameters.description: The UUID of the source resource
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The type of relationship of the objects; Uses the RelationshipType.representation value
parameters.name: relationshipType

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:uuid-values xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:uuids>df6de846-0e97-408d-bcff-be0026eba68a b3a3b7ef-7871-4238-86f4-3a5f10294450</ops:uuids>
</ops:uuid-values>

requestBody.description: UUID of resources to set as related to this resource

204.description: The relationship is set successfully

404.description: All of the Resource that are part of the relatedIds list are invalid/non-existent

put.summary: Set (Replace) Resource as RelationshipType of a specific Resource

delete.description:
NOTE: Removing a relationship is not synchronous. As a
result, the delete operation may not happen immediately. It is recommended
to query the relationships of the specific Resource back to ensure that the
operation was indeed successful.

parameters.description: The UUID of the resource
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Type of the Relationship that needs to be removed
parameters.name: relationshipType

parameters.description: The UUID of the resource to remove from relationship
parameters.name: relatedId

204.description: The relationship is deleted successfully

404.description: Either of the Resource that are part of the path parameters are invalid/non-existent

delete.summary: Delete (remove) a Resource as RelationshipType of a specific Resource

get.summary: Get all the stat keys for the specified Resource

get.description: NOTE: Unless a time range is explicitly set, the method returns statKeys which have received data in the last 24 hours.

parameters.description: Array of Resource ids for which the stats should be fetched
parameters.name: resourceId

parameters.description: Array of stat keys for which the stats should be returned
parameters.name: statKey

parameters.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used
parameters.name: begin

schema.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

parameters.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used
parameters.name: end

schema.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used

parameters.name: intervalQuantifier

schema.description: Number for the interval type

Indicates that the requested stats are based on some dynamic threshold.
Defaults to false which indicates that stats are not based on dynamic threshold.

schema.description:
Indicates that the requested stats are based on some dynamic threshold.
Defaults to false which indicates that stats are not based on dynamic threshold.

parameters.description:
The maximum number of samples to return when querying latest.
When processing the get latest stats query if it is omitted a value of 1 is assumed.
A query for the latest samples is not compatible with specifying the RollUpType value.
parameters.name: latestMaxSamples

schema.description:

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:symptom-definitions xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="CRITICAL">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>95</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="CRITICAL">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>70</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="WARNING">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>50</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="INFORMATION">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:numericPropertyCondition">
                <ops:operator>LT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:key>system|installed_memGB</ops:key>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
                <ops:value>8.0</ops:value>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="NONE">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:messageEventCondition" eventType="RESOURCE_DOWN" operator="CONTAINS">
                <ops:message>message event conditions are of eventType NOTIFICATION, RESOURCE_DOWN, SYSTEM_DEGRADATION, and compare message to event message via EQ or CONTAINS operator</ops:message>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="UNKNOWN">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:metricEventCondition">
                <ops:hardThresholdEventType>ABOVE</ops:hardThresholdEventType>
                <ops:statKey>cpu|demandmhz</ops:statKey>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="INFORMATION">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:faultCondition">
                <ops:faultKey>FaultKey - FaultKey matches to a FaultEvent's FaultState</ops:faultKey>
                <ops:faultEvents>FaultEvent1 FaultEvent2</ops:faultEvents>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">
        <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="CRITICAL">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>mem|host_usagePct</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>95</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>

                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>70</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">
        <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="WARNING">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>mem|host_usagePct</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>50</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>

        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">
        <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="INFORMATION">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:stringPropertySymptomCondition">
                <ops:operator>EQ</ops:operator>

    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">
        <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="WARNING">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:messageEventCondition" eventType="RESOURCE_DOWN" operator="CONTAINS">

            </ops:condition>

    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">

        <ops:state severity="WARNING">

get.summary: Get Symptom Definitions

post.description:
The symptom definition identifier must be null and will be generated by the system.
A symptom definition contains a symptom state, there may only be one symptom state per symptom definition.

<ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-45b0cc92-f082-4853-bce9-eeb09a3bcf9f" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:name>Log based symptom definition</ops:name>
    <ops:state severity="INFORMATION">
        <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:logCondition">
            <ops:logTriggerCondition>
                <ops:functionType>COUNT</ops:functionType>
                <ops:value>2.0</ops:value>
                <ops:operatorType>GREATER_THAN</ops:operatorType>
                <ops:timeInterval>5</ops:timeInterval>
                <ops:groupBy/>
            </ops:logTriggerCondition>
            <ops:queryTexts>
                <ops:queryText>filter_1</ops:queryText>
                <ops:queryText>filter_2</ops:queryText>
            </ops:queryTexts>
        </ops:condition>
    </ops:state>
</ops:symptom-definition>

Symptom Definition with HT Condition.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
    <ops:state severity="CRITICAL">
        <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
            <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
            <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
            <ops:value>95</ops:value>
            <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
            <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
            <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
        </ops:condition>
    </ops:state>
</ops:symptom-definition>

get.description:
Optionally these Symptoms can be looked up for a specific set of Resources and/or
can include Symptoms that are no longer active.
The list of Symptoms returned is a collection that supports pagination.

parameters.description: Array of resource identifiers for which Symptoms need to be looked up
parameters.name: resourceId

parameters.description: Boolean that determines whether the results should include inactive Symptoms
parameters.name: activeOnly

parameters.description: Boolean that controls the retrieval of alarm information of the symptom
parameters.name: includeAlarmInfo

parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page
parameters.name: pageSize

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:symptoms xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:symptom id="31eeaeec-82d5-4037-a59b-efed2e7c8e3a" kpi="false">
        <ops:resourceId>50000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000005</ops:resourceId>
        <ops:startTimeUTC>1753336526993</ops:startTimeUTC>
        <ops:updateTimeUTC>1753336526993</ops:updateTimeUTC>
        <ops:cancelTimeUTC>0</ops:cancelTimeUTC>
        <ops:symptomCriticality>INFORMATION</ops:symptomCriticality>
        <ops:symptomDefinitionId>SymptomDefinition-1</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
        <ops:message>symptom message</ops:message>
        <ops:faultDevices/>
    </ops:symptom>
    <ops:symptom id="18589dea-9999-1234-abba-f1ee73004dac" kpi="false">
        <ops:resourceId>50000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000005</ops:resourceId>
        <ops:startTimeUTC>1753336526993</ops:startTimeUTC>
        <ops:updateTimeUTC>1753336526993</ops:updateTimeUTC>
        <ops:cancelTimeUTC>0</ops:cancelTimeUTC>
        <ops:symptomCriticality>INFORMATION</ops:symptomCriticality>
        <ops:symptomDefinitionId>SymptomDefinition-1</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
        <ops:message>symptom message</ops:message>
        <ops:faultDevices/>
    </ops:symptom>
</ops:symptoms>

</ops:symptom-query>

get.description:
In general, to get the status of any long running operations, the operation
must have been initiated through the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations REST API.
This API endpoint returns the details of the long running tasks that were submitted via the API only.
Furthermore, the list of tasks that this API returns includes tasks for long running operations such
as Adapter Installation, Describe etc. This API does not return the tasks created when any
Remediation Actions are executed in the system either via the UI or via the API.

parameters.description: States of the tasks
parameters.name: taskState

schema.description: States of the tasks

parameters.description: The task ids
parameters.name: taskId

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:task-statuses xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:task-status>
        <ops:taskId>bf41869d-1a6e-48de-8ba5-26399ca34de9</ops:taskId>
        <ops:statusMessage>Distributed Task Execution for ADAPTER_INSTALL</ops:statusMessage>
        <ops:taskState>RUNNING</ops:taskState>
        <ops:createdTime>0</ops:createdTime>
        <ops:lastUpdateTime>1753340192459</ops:lastUpdateTime>
        <ops:errorMessages/>
    </ops:task-status>
</ops:task-statuses>

get.summary: Look up a list of task objects' statuses

get.description:
In general, to get the status of any long running operations, the operation
must have been initiated through the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations REST API.
This API returns the status of a specific task that was initiated via the REST API
with the exception of tasks that get created when any Remediation Action is performed
via the api/actions endpoint.

parameters.description: The id of the task
parameters.name: id

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:task-status xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:taskId>bf41869d-1a6e-48de-8ba5-26399ca34de9</ops:taskId>
    <ops:statusMessage>Distributed Task Execution for ADAPTER_INSTALL</ops:statusMessage>
    <ops:taskState>RUNNING</ops:taskState>
    <ops:createdTime>0</ops:createdTime>
    <ops:lastUpdateTime>1753340192459</ops:lastUpdateTime>
    <ops:errorMessages/>
</ops:task-status>

200.description: Task status with the specified identifier

404.description: No Task is found with the specified identifier

get.summary: Look up a task status with the given identifier

ResourceFilter.description:
Filter for resources. If matchResourceIdOnly is specified, alerts are selected if their resource ids
match with the 'resourceId' specified in the filter. Otherwise, the system looks at all the resource's children.
The possible set of children can be further filtered based on the childrenResourceKindKeyFilters.

childrenResourceKindFilters.description:
If matchResourceIdOnly is set to false, this set allows the system to further filter children
based on their Resource Kind keys. This filter is deprecated. Please use childrenResourceKindKeyFilters instead.

items.description:
If matchResourceIdOnly is set to false, this set allows the system to further filter children
based on their Resource Kind keys. This filter is deprecated. Please use childrenResourceKindKeyFilters instead.

childrenResourceKindKeyFilters.description: Filter based on children resource kind keys.

matchResourceIdOnly.description:
Deprecated, will be removed after couple of releases. Please use relationship instead.
If set to true, the rule engine does not evaluate the resource's children. Default value is: false.
NOTE: If set to true, childrenResourceKindKeyFilters has to be empty.

relationship.description:

action-context.description: Represents an executable step within an action. It defines which method the step calls and what fields related to this step.

fields.description: The action context fields defined for the action with data populated using the expression where defined

id.description: The ID of the action context

action-context-field.description: Defines a field within the action context of an action. This field may be a display only parameter, and may have an expression that determines its value or the default one.

component.description: The display component appropriate for action context field

displayOrder.description: The order of this field within the list of all action context fields

expressionResults.description: The result of running the expression on each resource, this first resource will be the first value in the list and the second will be the second value, etc.

items.description: The result of running the expression on each resource, this first resource will be the first value in the list and the second will be the second value, etc.

hidden.description: True if this action context field may be hidden from display

id.description: The ID of the action context field

input.description: True if user can change the populated value of this field, false otherwise

name.description: The name of the action context field

parameterKey.description: The parameter key on the method to which this action context field corresponds

actionAdapterKindKey.description: The kind of the adapter that will execute the action

canRecommend.description: This field specifies whether the action can be used in recommendations. This is validated when an action is associated to a recommendation.

contextAdapterKindKey.description: The adapter kind of the context object that the action will be populated with. In other words, the kind of adapter that will be used when populating an action prior to execution. This may be different from the target resource, or the kind of the adapter that the action is executed against

contextIds.description: The ids for the available Action Contexts for this action. Action Contexts are used in multi-step actions, with each id denoting a step in the overall action. Most actions will only have a single step, and this field may be ignored for those.

items.description: The ids for the available Action Contexts for this action. Action Contexts are used in multi-step actions, with each id denoting a step in the overall action. Most actions will only have a single step, and this field may be ignored for those.

contextResourceKindKey.description: The resource kind of the context object that the action will be populated with. In other words, the kind of resource that will be used when populating an action prior to execution. This may be different than the kind of resource that the action is executed against, or the target resource.

displayName.description: The localized name of the action

scheduleEnabled.description: This field specifies whether the action can be scheduled

alert-definition.description:
Defines a Problem by associating the Criticality level (Criticality)
of Symptom Definitions on related objects in the AlertDefinitionState
and also associates to a particular impact (AlertDefinitionImpact)

states.description:
An Alert Definition state identifies the severity and impact a problem has when the
set of Symptom Definition conditions are true. One Alert Definition state per severity.

The sub type of the problem, for the purposes of routing an alert
generated by this problem.

type.description:
The type of the problem, for the purposes of routing an alert generated
by this problem.

alert-definition-impact.description: The alert's impact on the system

alert-definition-state.description:
The problem state describes the severity of a problem when specific symptoms exist on related resources. It optionally may include a prioritized recommendation list.
The Symptom Set can be either a {@link SymptomSet} or {@link CompositeSymptomSet}.

additionalProperties.description:
A map of recommendation identifiers keyed by recommendation id. A lower
priority value indicates higher priority. The priorities are required to
start from 1.

recommendationPriorityMap.description:
A map of recommendation identifiers keyed by recommendation id. A lower
priority value indicates higher priority. The priorities are required to
start from 1.

auth-source.description:
Besides local users, VMware Cloud Foundation Operations also allows users from external systems such as LDAP, Active Directory, VC, SSO to
login using their original credentials. Authentication of all such users are delegated to external systems. This
model represents a single auth source managed by VMware Cloud Foundation Operations.

certificates.description:
Certificate details of the Authentication Source.
Typically this will be present if and only if the Authentication Source enforces SSL

created.description: The Auth Source creation/import time in milliseconds

id.description: Immutable Identifier of the Auth Source

lastModified.description: The Auth Source last modification time in millisecond

name.description:
Name of the External System. Set by admin when the source is added or imported into vROps.
name and id of a source is unique with the exception that name can be modified by a system admin.

property.description:
Properties of the Authentication Source.
The actual values of the metadata elements relevant for the specific Authentication Source

auth-source-property.description: Meta data about auth source property

dataType.description: The data type of the property

modifiable.description: Flag that indicates whether the property can be modified during an update operation or not

required.description: Flag that indicates whether property is required or not for creating/updating an auth source

Represents the collection of Authentication Sources
