capacityAllocationSettings.description: Collection of capacity allocation model settings.

capacity-allocation-settings.description:
Allocation Model differs to Demand Model as it does not consider Utilization. It is simply based on configured amount (e.g. 4 vCPU, 16 GB virtual RAM, 100 GB virtual Disk) that is allocated to VMs. As a result, Demand Model is still considered when Allocation is enabled.
Note: These settings will also apply to What-If Analysis, Committed Projects, and WLP calculations.

demand.description: Demand value. This field applicable only for Cluster Compute Resource

usage.description: Usage Value. This field applicable only for Datastore and Datastore Cluster

capacity-buffer-settings.description: Capacity buffer reduces the usable capacity. In vSphere Cluster, it is applied after High Availability (HA). Use buffer as safety net in capacity.

items.description: Collection of custom profile uuids.

certificate.description: If the source returns an untrusted certificate, this field is used to represent the full certificate. Currently used only for VIDB and provided in PEM format.

certificateDetails.description: If the source returns an untrusted certificate, this field is used to represent certificate details. It also contains Algorithm, Signature (base64-encoded) and SignedCertificate (base64-encoded) values.

issuedBy.description: The issuedBy parameter of the certificate

issuedTo.description: The issuedTo date of the certificate

thumbprint.description: If the source returns an untrusted certificate,this field is used to represent the certificate guid

certificates.description: List of untrusted certificates, provided for acceptance.

certificates-error.description: Error Message that is returned as part of HTTP response in case of certificates issue, during adapter connection testing.

capacityRemainingSettings.description: Capacity Remaining is the not consumed percentage of usable capacity.

timeRemainingSettings.description: Time Remaining is how many days you have until the utilization projection crosses the usable capacity threshold. It has 2 settings: Conservative and Aggressive.

workloadSettings.description: Workload is the immediate % of capacity consumed of the most constrained of several key resource containers. Since workload changes every collection cycle, you can set how many cycles it takes to trigger or clear an alert.

critical.description: Threshold for critical. It is null, when criticality level is disabled.

immediate.description: Threshold for immediate. It is null, when criticality level is disabled.

warning.description: Threshold for warning. It is null, when criticality level is disabled.

crossDCMoveAllowed.description: Specifies whether cross datacenter move allowed or not. This is applicable only for custom datacenters

Visit the ISO web site for more information.

Collection of resources to exclude from the given group. This property is useful when the group
membership is dynamic and you want to explicitly exclude certain set of resources even if they
are selected by the rule engine.

Collection of resources to exclude from the given group. This property is useful when the group

are selected by the rule engine.

dt-stat-query.description:
Object used to define search criteria for retrieving Stats Data as well as Dynamic Thresholds
The query spec enforces that at least one Resource (Object) and at least one StatKey is specified.
The begin and end values that are used to specify the time window are optional. If
these are not specified the API returns 24 hours of Stats and DT data by default.

begin.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC

end.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC

resourceIds.description: Collection of Resource identifiers for which Stats and DT values need to be retrieved

items.description: Collection of Resource identifiers for which Stats and DT values need to be retrieved

showSmooth.description: Flag that indicates whether stats should be smoothened or not. Default value is true

smoothFactor.description: The smooth precision, default value is 2.0

statKeys.description: Collection of StatKeys for which Stats and DT values need to be retrieved

items.description: Collection of StatKeys for which Stats and DT values need to be retrieved

If those fields are not provided, the value of newAlertTemplate will be used while sending the notification.

event.description: Class to represent external events.  Different event types have different required fields.

cancelTimeUTC.description:
Indicates that an event has been cancelled. For an externally managed event a new event with
the same message value must be sent with a cancelTime in order to cancel the
event.

eventType.description:
Represents the type of an Event.
All values are valid for the field eventType in the Event object.
When used in the creation of a SymptomDefinition the following restrictions are
applicable for the Conditions that are defined as part of a Symptom Definition.

htType.description: The type of Metric Event or Metric Event Condition

key.description: The StatKey for the condition of the HardThreshold event

keyIndicator.description:
Whether the HardThreshold event is a key performance indicator
True indicates it is a KPI. False otherwise.

managedExternally.description:
If set to true, this event shall persist across collection cycles until another event is pushed
to change the status.
If set to false, this event will be cancelled during the next collection cycle if it is not pushed again

message.description: Event message, the message identifies the event in the system.

resourceId.description: The id of the resource for the event

startTimeUTC.description:
The start time for the event in UTC.
This time must be after >= 946684800000L, or January 1, 2000. Default is 946684800000L.
Value is ignored for Change events.

value.description: The current value (not the expected value) of the metric for the HardThreshold event

fault-alarm-device.description: Additional information of the faulted devices.

faultEvents.description:
The list of fault faultEvents that we are interested in tracking.
The symptom definition this condition is part of will be triggered if any of the fault faultEvents occurred.

items.description:
The list of fault faultEvents that we are interested in tracking.
The symptom definition this condition is part of will be triggered if any of the fault faultEvents occurred.

faultKey.description: The fault condition faultKey matches the Fault Event's FaultState

imported.description: The number of objects in content

skipped.description: The number of skipped objects in content

certificates.description: List of new certificates for this integration, that need to be accepter for further operations.

key-value.description: Model to handle arbitrary key/value entries, with XML bindings

latest-stat-query.description:
Object used to request latest stats (metrics) on a collection of resources.
Defines search criteria as well as expected data set.

currentOnly.description:
Indicates whether to report only "current" stat values, i.e. skip the stat-s that haven't published any value
during recent collection cycles.

maxSamples.description:
The maximum number of samples to return when querying latest stats.
Defaults to 1 so that when processing the query if it is omitted
the response would contain a single sample

metrics.description: Indicates whether specified statKey-s of query should be forcibly treated as metrics (does not include instanced stats)

resourceId.description: Array of Resource ids for which the latest stats should be fetched

items.description: Array of Resource ids for which the latest stats should be fetched

statKey.description: Array of stat keys for which the latest stats should be returned

items.description: Array of stat keys for which the latest stats should be returned

wrapStatValues.description:
Indicates whether stat values should be wrapped for application/xml response content type.
This flag is false by default and not needed for application/json response.
If set to true "statValues" will be returned instead of "values" for all content types.

link.description:
Represents a HTTP 'link' element.
It is implicit that all the link tags support http 'GET' method.

href.description:
The url part of this tag. Note that the href can be absolute or relative to the current url.
If the href begins with '/', then its absolute.
If the href begins with the protocol element(http, https), its absolute
Otherwise its relative.

rel.description: Represents the link's relation to its parent tag/object

localization.description: Localized names of the unit for all localizations

language.description: The language of the localized value

longDescription.description: Long descriptions of the localized value

items.description: Long descriptions of the localized value

shortDescription.description: Short descriptions of the localized value

items.description: Short descriptions of the localized value

autoCancelTime.description: The time after which the cancel event will be received from Log Insight.

queryId.description: The queryId will be set for 'Saved' queries. For 'Custom' queries, it will be null.

queryTexts.description: The text contains query checks if a queryTexts is present within a given text.

items.description: The text contains query checks if a queryTexts is present within a given text.

maxLogSize.description: This number specifies how much of log data to retain (MB)

rootLoggerLevel.description: The level of the root log

log-forwarding-configuration.description: Represents the Log Forwarding Configuration object

enabled.description: Indicates whether the logs should go to external log server or not

entities.description: Entities to forward the logs from

items.description: Enumeration of the Log Entity

host.description: The host to forward the logs to

port.description:
The Forwarding port


The port takes default value based on the protocol and whether SSL is enabled or not:
Protocol CFAPI, SSL disabled, Default port: 9000
Protocol CFAPI, SSL enabled, Default port: 9543
Protocol SYSLOG, SSL disabled, Default port: 514
Protocol SYSLOG, SSL enabled, Default port: 6514

sslCertPath.description: The path to certificate authority file

useSSL.description: Flag that indicates whether SSL protocol is used or not

items.description: Collection of condition values
Collection of partition

log-query-filters-operator.description: Operator type for query filters. Default value is AND.

field.description: Field for trigger condition. This field should be null when the function type is COUNT

timeInterval.description: Time interval for the trigger condition. Values are in minutes. Allowed values are {5, 15, 30, 60, 360}

maintenance-schedule.description: Represents a maintenance schedule during which resources are set to 'maintained' state. The server inturn informs the adapters that monitor these resources to stop monitoring until specified otherwise.Each maintenance schedule has an id, key and schedule. Both id and key have to be unique within the system. While id is automatically assigned by the server, key must be specified by the user.

id.description: Identifier generated by the server for this schedule.

key.description: Schedule key provided by user. Cannot be null during creation.

membership-rule-group.description:
Group membership rule. Requires the resource kind key to specify a resource kind to be included in the group.
Optionally the included resources can be filtered by metric key/property rules, relationship rules or resource name rules.

propertyConditionRules.description: Collection of property conditions for a custom group

relationshipConditionRules.description: Collection of relationship rules for a custom group

resourceNameConditionRules.description: Collection of resource name rules for a custom group

resourceTagConditionRules.description: Collection of resource tag rules for a custom group

statConditionRules.description: Collection of metric conditions for a custom group

eventType.description:
Represents the type of an Event.
All values are valid for the field eventType in the Event object.
When used in the creation of a SymptomDefinition the following restrictions are
applicable for the Conditions that are defined as part of a Symptom Definition.

message.description:
The message is the string that the event's message must
either match exactly if operator is CompareOperator.EQ or
be a substring if operator is CompareOperator.CONTAINS
NOTE: Also in this case the message must be 3-128 characters long and the
symptom from the event must be IN the message

operator.description: Comparison Operator used within the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations Suite API

message-event-condition.description: Message Event Condition used as part of a Symptom Definition for the Event type

assignedToTenant.description: Indicates bill assignment to the tenant

policyName.description: The policyName used for the Bill

totalPrice.description: The total price associated with the resource

hardThresholdEventType.description: The type of Metric Event or Metric Event Condition

metric-event-condition.description:
Model object for a Metric Event Condition.
Implicitly maps to EventType#HARD_THRESHOLD.
The operator only allows for EXISTS conditions so specification of an operator
is not necessary and CompareOperator.EXISTS is implicitly used.

name-value.description: Represents a single name -> value tuple.

pluginId.description: The Plugin Id, cannot be null during update operations

notification-rule.description:
Model that represents a notification rule.
VMware Cloud Foundation Operations has a rule engine that evaluates these rules against generated alerts.
Actions such as sending Email, HTTP callback is done based on matching rules.
NOTE: resourceFilter and resourceKindFilter fields are deprecated, instead please use resourceFilters and resourceKindFilters fields respectively.

actionStatuses.description: Set of Action statuses that the action is expected to be in

items.description: Indicates the final status of an action.

alertControlStates.description: Set of Alert control states that the alert is expected to be in

items.description: The control state of an Alert

alertStatuses.description: Set of Alert states that the alert is expected to be in

items.description:
Indicates the current condition of an Alert.
Used to determine if the conditions for the Alert still continue to exist.

alertTypeFilters.description: Filter for the rule to match Alerts which are of the specified Alert type & Alert Subtypes

collectorGroupId.description: Filter for the rule to match Alerts triggered on resources belonging to given collector group

collectorUUId.description: Filter for the rule to match Alerts triggered on resources belonging to given collector

criticalities.description: Set of Alert criticalities that the alert is expected to be in

items.description: The Criticality level

enabled.description: Identifier that shows if the notification rule is enabled. Enabled by default

id.description: Identifier of this rule, automatically assigned by the system on create

pluginId.description: Identifier of the plugin for which this rule applies

properties.description:
Properties associated with the given rule. This may include information such as the email addresses of
receivers/etc...

resourceFilters.description: Filters for the rule to be matched based on the "Resource and its Children" for which the Alerts were generated. This collection takes precedence over resourceFilter field.

resourceKindFilters.description: Filter for the rule to be matched based on the Resource Kind for which the alert was generated

sendHeartbeat.description: Filter for the rule to send Alerts while they are active. Defaulted to False if nothing is provided.

templateId.description:
Identifier of the template.
NOTE: For deprecated plugin types this field will be ignored. For all other plugin types, default templates will be used if this value is not specified.

lastModifiedOn.description: Last time the template is modified

Represents the Property Content posted by the user.
The data posted by the user can be numeric data or string data.
However both types of the data cannot be sent together.

items.description: The array of numeric data that is being posted

statKey.description: Stat Key for which the data is being posted

timestamps.description: The array of timestamps at which the data is being posted. All the timestamps must be in milliseconds.

recommendation.description:
The Recommendation encapsulates information and possible action needed to resolve a problem in the system.
The Recommendation optionally includes a RecommendedAction.

In other words, a recommendation may have textual data and no associated action
or it can have both.
The Recommendation is uniquely identified by an identifier
which is generated by the system during its creation.

id.description: The recommendation id. Must be null during creation (System generated identifier)

recommended-action.description: Identifies an action the system may take to solve a problem.

actionAdapterKindId.description:
The Adapter Kind that defined this Recommended Action
An example could be the Out-of-the-box Remediation Adapter Kind ID

targetAdapterKindId.description:
The Adapter Kind for which this Recommended Action is applicable.
An example could be the Out-of-the-box VC Adapter Kind ID viz. "VMWARE"

targetMethod.description:
Name of the remediation action that needs to be performed on the resource
An example could be a few of the VM lifecycle operations viz. "shutdown", "reset" etc.

targetResourceKindId.description:
Resource Kind of the Adapter Kind targetAdapterKindId
on which this Recommended Action is applicable.
For certain recommended actions, this can be null.
The system supports a scenario where the Recommended Action is only
associated with targetAdapterKindId only.
An example could be the Out-of-the-box VC Resource Kind ID viz. "VirtualMachine"

credentialInstanceId.description: Credential instance identifier assigned to this Resource, applicable only for adapter instances.

resources-stat-contents.description: Represents a Collection of Resources and Stats associated with each Resource.

resource-stat-content.description: Collection of Resources and the associated Stats

role-permission.description: Represents role based permissions to objects.  The role's privileges will apply to all of the objects identified by the traversal specification. Optionally permission can be flagged to allow all objects ignoring traversal specifications.

allowAllObjects.description: If true then ignore traversal specifications and allow all objects

schedule.description: Schedule represents the meta information required by the server to set resources in maintenance mode at the right interval/time. To construct specific schedules, please see some of the static helper utilities in this class.

dayOfTheMonth.description:
Deprecated, will be removed after couple of releases.
Day of the month on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with daysOfTheMonth and weeksOfTheMonth.

daysOfTheMonth.description: The list of the days of the month on which the job should be triggered. Applicable values are {1,2,...,31,LAST}. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with dayOfTheMonth and weeksOfTheMonth.

items.description: The list of the days of the month on which the job should be triggered. Applicable values are {1,2,...,31,LAST}. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with dayOfTheMonth and weeksOfTheMonth.

daysOfTheWeek.description:
Days of the week on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = WEEKLY or scheduleType = MONTHLY.
When scheduleType = MONTHLY this parameter is mutually exclusive with dayOfTheMonth and daysOfTheMonth, should be specified together with daysOfTheWeek.

items.description: Days of the Week

duration.description: Duration of the schedule in minutes.

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the schedule. Should be in format MM/DD/YYYY. Mutually exclusive with expireRuns and either of them should be always defined when scheduleType != ONCE.

expireRuns.description: The number of run after which the schedule should expire. Mutually exclusive with expirationDate and either of them should be always defined when scheduleType != ONCE.

hour.description: The beginning hour of the schedule.

minuteOfTheHour.description: The beginning minutes of the schedule.

month.description:
Deprecated, will be removed after couple of releases.
Month of the year on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with months.

months.description: Months of the year on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with month.

items.description: Months of the year on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with month.

recurrence.description:
Configures how the schedule is repeated within the provided interval, e.g. if scheduleType = DAILY then setting recurrence = 2 would trigger the job every 2 days.
Applicable only when scheduleType = DAILY or scheduleType = WEEKLY.

startDate.description: Start date for the schedule. Should be in format MM/DD/YYYY and if not specified current date is used.

timeZone.description:
Time Zone of the schedule.
If not provided, defaults to the time of the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations server.

weeksOfTheMonth.description: The list of the week indices of the month on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with dayOfTheMonth and daysOfTheMonth, should be specified together with daysOfTheWeek.

items.description: Week of the Month

scope.description: Represents a VMware Cloud Foundation Operations Manager system.

id.description: Identifier of the Scope in the system.

scope-details.description: Represents a Scope VMware Cloud Foundation Operations Manager system.

createdBy.description: The user id who created the scope.

id.description: Identifier of the scope in the system.

lastModifiedBy.description: The user id who last modified the scope.

lastModifiedTime.description: Last Modification time of the scope

service.description: Model that represents a Service running as part of the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations stack.

details.description: An optional message field that provides details especially in case of errors

name.description: List of Services that are part of the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations stack

startedBy.description: OS user that started the Service

startedOn.description: When the Service was started (Epoch time in milliseconds)

uptime.description: Uptime of the Service (Epoch time in milliseconds)

isActivated.description: Flag that indicates whether to activate configuration or no

parameters.description: Collection of service configurations

configNames.description: Collection of configurations to be deleted

items.description: Collection of configurations to be deleted

serviceName.description: The name of the application service
The name of applications service configuration

service.description: Collection of services along with their health status

adapterKindKeys.description: List of Adapter Kind keys associated with the Solution

items.description: List of Adapter Kind keys associated with the Solution

solutions.description: Model to represent a collection of solutions

source-target.description: Represents source target details of VM motion.

stat-content.description:
Represents the Stat Content posted by the user.
The data posted by the user can be numeric data or string data.
However both types of the data cannot be sent together.

data.description: The numeric data that is being posted

items.description: The numeric data that is being posted

statKey.description: Stat Key for which the data is being posted

timestamps.description: The array of timestamps at which the data is being posted. All the timestamps must be in milliseconds.

items.description: The array of timestamps at which the data is being posted. All the timestamps must be in milliseconds.

values.description: The string values that are being posted

items.description: The string values that are being posted

stat-contents.description: Represents the Stat Contents posted by the user

stat-key.description: Unique representation of a metric-key/stat stored and exposed by the stats platform

key.description: The stat key

stat-keys.description: Container for a List of StatKey values

stat-list.description: The stats for the corresponding resource id

stat-or-property-condition.description: Model that represents a Property Condition

doubleValue.description: Double value to which we need to compare to

key.description: The name of the StatKey or Property to which the condition applies

operator.description: Comparison Operator used within the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations Suite API

stringValue.description: String value to which we need to compare to

stat-or-property-condition-query.description: Object used to define filtering criteria based on multiple stats/properties with possibility to specify the conjunction operator

conditions.description: Array of condition to be used for filtering

stat-query.description:
Object used to request stats(metrics) on a collection of resources.
Defines search criteria as well as expected data set.

begin.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

currentOnly.description:
Indicates whether to report only "current" stat values, i.e. skip the stat-s that haven't published any value
during recent collection cycles.

dt.description:
Indicates that the requested stats are based on some dynamic threshold.
Defaults to false which indicates that stats are not based on dynamic threshold.

end.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used

latestMaxSamples.description:
The maximum number of samples to return when querying latest.
When processing the get latest stats query if it is omitted a value of 1 is assumed.
A query for the latest samples is not compatible with specifying the RollUpType value.

metrics.description: Indicates whether specified statKey-s of query should be forcibly treated as metrics (does not include instanced stats)

resourceId.description: Array of Resource ids for which the stats should be fetched

items.description: Array of Resource ids for which the stats should be fetched

statKey.description: Array of stat keys for which the stats should be returned

items.description: Array of stat keys for which the stats should be returned

wrapStatValues.description:
Indicates whether stat values should be wrapped for application/xml response content type.
This flag is false by default and not needed for application/json response.
If set to true "statValues" will be returned instead of "values" for all content types.

items.description: The numeric data

dtTimestamps.description: Time stamps at which dt thresholds data is available

items.description: Time stamps at which dt thresholds data is available

maxThresholdData.description: Data for available max dt thresholds timestamps

items.description: Data for available max dt thresholds timestamps

minThresholdData.description: Data for available min dt thresholds timestamps

items.description: Data for available min dt thresholds timestamps

rollUpType.description: Represents the type of Rollup required for the stats data

items.description: The smooth values

statValues.description:
The string data wrapped.
This is equivalent to the "values" field, with the sole difference of having values wrapped in separate elements when content type is application/xml.

items.description:
The string data wrapped.
This is equivalent to the "values" field, with the sole difference of having values wrapped in separate elements when content type is application/xml.

timestamps.description: Time stamps at which data is available

items.description: Time stamps at which data is available
The string data

name.description: Name of storage rate policy. Will be ignored if it is marked as default.

stringValue.description: The string value for the condition

sub-metering-bill.description: The sub metering items of the bill

meteringDetails.description: The metering details of the sub metering items of the bill

subMeterings.description: The sub metering items of the bill

sub-meterings.description: The sub metering items of the bill

key.description: The resource kind of the sub metering

values.description: The sub meterings of the resource kind

supermetric.description: Model object that represents a SuperMetric

formula.description: The formula expression of the SuperMetric

id.description:
The unique ID of the SuperMetric. This will be null when creating a
SuperMetric, but it is required when updating an existing SuperMetric.

modificationTime.description: Last modification time of the SuperMetric

alarmInfo.description:
Additional information of the symptom.
NOTE: General recommendation is not to rely on the current format of the message content
since it can be changed by the source of alarm information at any time.
So, backward compatibility of the content of the message format is not guaranteed.

cancelTimeUTC.description: Time when the symptom was canceled

faultDevices.description: Additional information of the faulted devices.

id.description: The ID which identifies the Symptom

kpi.description: True, if the symptom is a KPI, false otherwise

message.description:
The message associated with a symptom.
This is usually applicable for Event based Symptoms

resourceId.description: The ID of the resource on which the Symptom was generated

symptomDefinitionId.description: The Symptom Definition describing the violated rule for this Symptom

updateTimeUTC.description: Last updated time of the symptom

symptom-definition.description: A Symptom Definition defines conditions and properties which define when an alarm is triggered

adapterKindKey.description: The adapter kind identifier for the SymptomDefinition

cancelCycles.description: The number of consecutive cycles to wait when a symptom state is false before a symptom is cancelled

id.description: The identifier for the SymptomDefinition. Must be null during create.

realtimeMonitoringEnabled.description: Indicates whether real-time monitoring is enabled for the symptom or not. (This field is not applicable)

resourceKindKey.description: The resource kind identifier for the SymptomDefinition

waitCycles.description: The number of consecutive cycles to wait when a symptom state is true before a symptom is triggered

symptom-query.description:
Query for Symptoms using various criteria.
Composition of filtering criteria controlled via the compositeOperator property.
Pagination of the Symptoms that are returned can be controlled via the PageInfo object.
Sorting of the Symptoms is specified via the SymptomField object and the SortOrder
object controls the order in which the Symptoms are sorted.
If a sort field is not specified but pagination is requested the Symptoms will be sorted
by the alarmId property.

activeOnly.description:
Include Symptoms that are currently Active.
By default, only active Symptoms are included in the result

alarmCriticality.description: Return Symptoms that have the specified list of Symptom Criticalities

items.description: The Criticality level

alarmType.description: Return Symptoms that have the specified list of Symptom Types

items.description: Return Symptoms that have the specified list of Symptom Types

includeAlarmInfo.description: Boolean that controls the retrieval of alarm information of the symptom

includeChildrenResources.description: Whether to include Symptoms of all the children of resources identified by resourceIds

statKey.description: Return Symptoms that have been generated because of violations on the Stat Keys specified

items.description: Return Symptoms that have been generated because of violations on the Stat Keys specified

symptomDefinitionId.description: Return Symptoms that have the specified list of Symptom Definitions

items.description: Return Symptoms that have the specified list of Symptom Definitions

symptomId.description: Return Symptoms that have the specified list of Symptom identifiers

items.description: Return Symptoms that have the specified list of Symptom identifiers

adapterKindKey.description: The adapter kind key of the Adapter Kind that the symptom definitions and conditions applies to

aggregation.description:
How a population condition examines the data across the population to generate a value.
It answers the question: How many resources of the specified relation should exhibit the symptoms?

populationOperator.description: Comparison Operator used within the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations Suite API

relation.description:
Relationship Types between resources. All relationships are many-to-many.
Representation names are plural.

resourceKindKey.description: The resource kind key for the Resource Kind that the symptom definitions and conditions applies to

symptomDefinitionIds.description: Identifier of the Symptom Definition to which this object refers.The reference can be negated to indicate a NOT EXISTS reference by prefixing the symptomDefinitionReference with the "!" operator .

items.description: Identifier of the Symptom Definition to which this object refers.The reference can be negated to indicate a NOT EXISTS reference by prefixing the symptomDefinitionReference with the "!" operator .

traversalSpecType.description: Specifies an optional traversal spec type by name

symptom-set.description: A reference to the symptoms by symptomDefinitionReferences which is full of symptom definition id values, on a related resource found by the relation.

symptom-state.description: The SymptomState is a product of the severity and a condition.  A symptom definition may have one SymptomState per severity level.

count.description: The number of objects in the audit

chargeOnPowerState.description: Indicates policy pricing charge on powered state settings

key.description: key of tag base rate policy

value.description: value of tag base rate policy

key.description: key of tag base rate

type.description: Used to identify schema type. For each schema its equivalent value should be used. allowableValues = {vc-tag-based-rate-factor}

tag-group.description: Represents the data for tag group settings

prioritized.description: Specifies whether prioritization is enabled for the group or not

enabled.description: By enabling this setting WLP will generate target network mapping automatically for moving VM workloads.

target-network-settings.description: Model representing generate target network mapping settings.

task-state.description: The state of an asynchronous Task

createdTime.description: Task status creation time, in milliseconds (since midnight January 1, 1970)

errorMessages.description: List of error messages that occurred

items.description: List of error messages that occurred

lastUpdateTime.description: Task status last update time, in milliseconds (since midnight January 1, 1970)

taskState.description: The state of an asynchronous Task

template-content-config.description: Model that represents a template content config.

ancestorResourceKindKeys.description: Ancestor resource kind keys and stats selected for them

descendantResourceKindKeys.description: Descendant resource kind keys and stats selected for them

template-property.description: Model that represents a notification template property.

key.description: Property key

type.description: Enumerated type for template property

time-range.description: Filter based on matching UTC cancel time

traversal-spec.description: Represents traversal specs. These are templates in the system using which admins can assign specific set of resources to their users.

adapterInstanceAssociation.description: When true the traversal spec templates applies to adapter kind associations.  A traversal spec instance based on this template must specify the included adapter kinds it applies to.

description.description: The description of the traversal spec

name.description: The name of the traversal spec

rootAdapterKindKey.description: The adapter kind of the root resource

rootResourceKindKey.description: The resource kind of the root resource.

traversal-spec-instance.description: Represents traversal spec type. This can be used by users and user groups in a permission objects.

adapterKind.description: The root Adapter kind of the Traversal Specification. Together with resourceKind and name these values uniquely identify the traversal specification used by this instance.

includedAdapterKinds.description: The adapter kinds that apply to this traversal specification instance. Only applicable for traversal specification templates with a filter type of ADAPTER_ASSOCIATION. If this is present, then all adapter instances of this kind are selected as part of the hierarchy.

items.description: The adapter kinds that apply to this traversal specification instance. Only applicable for traversal specification templates with a filter type of ADAPTER_ASSOCIATION. If this is present, then all adapter instances of this kind are selected as part of the hierarchy.

name.description: The name of the Traversal Specification. Together with adapterKind and resourceKind these values uniquely identify a traversal specification used by this instance.

resourceKind.description: The root Resource kind of the Traversal Specification. Together with adapterKind and name these values uniquely identify a traversal specification used by this instance.

resourceSelection.description: List of root resource id selections

selectAllResources.description: When flag is true system will ignore resource selections and include all resources in the tree defined by the traversal specification templat.

traversal-specs.description: Traversal Specs applicable for the Report Definition

user.description: Represents a local user of VMware Cloud Foundation Operations system.

distinguishedName.description: distinguished name of the current user.

emailAddress.description: The email address of the current user

enabled.description: Is the current user's account enabled?

externalId.description: External ID of vIDB user. This field is only used in vIDB user import flow. It will not be populated for any other scenarios. Please note, this field may be removed in future.

firstName.description: First name of the current user.

groupIds.description: The list of group ids to which the current user belongs to.

items.description: The list of group ids to which the current user belongs to.

id.description: Identifier of the user in the system.

lastName.description: The last name of the current user.

password.description: Password of the user object. When creating a new local user, this field has to be specified.

role-permissions.description: List of role permissions assigned to this user. May be null. Replaces and takes precedence over roleNames

roleNames.description: (Deprecated) List of role names assigned to the user. May be null.

items.description: (Deprecated) List of role names assigned to the user. May be null.

authSourceId.description: The identifier of the auth source. If it is null, a local user group will be created.

externalId.description: External ID of vIDB user group. This field is only used in vIDB user group import flow. It will not be populated for any other scenarios. Please note, this field may be removed in future.

id.description:
The identifier of this group.(unique)

Note: Required when updating or deleting the user group.

role-permissions.description: The list of role permissions assigned to this group. May be null. Replaces and takes precedence over roleNames.

roleNames.description: (Deprecated) List of role names assigned to the user group.  May be null.

items.description: (Deprecated) List of role names assigned to the user group.  May be null.

userIds.description: The list of users who are members of this group.

items.description: The list of users who are members of this group.

user-role.description: Represents a role object in the system.

displayName.description: A role name suitable for display.

items.description: The collection of privilege keys

system-created.description: True if this role was created by the system

user-search-request.description: The request object to search users

domain.description: The auth source domain. Mandatory for an sso auth source

name.description: The name of the user to be searched. Can contain wildcards

externalId.description: External ID of vIDB user. This field is only used in vIDB user import flow. It will not be populated for any other scenarios. Please note, this field may be removed in future.

usergroup-search-request.description: The request object to search user groups

domain.description: The auth source domain. Mandatory for an sso auth source

name.description: The name of the user group to be searched. Can contain wildcards

usergroup-search-response.description: The object contains user group details

distinguishedName.description: Distinguished name of the user group

externalId.description: External ID of vIDB user group. This field is only used in vIDB user group import flow. It will not be populated for any other scenarios. Please note, this field may be removed in future.

username-password.description: Represents model of Username and Password

items.description: Represents a list of UUID(s)

validationFailure.description: Validation failure is returned back to user if the input data is not valid

violationPath.description: XML/JSON path that points to the location of the invalid value/error

vc-guest-os-rate-settings.description: Model representing guest OS rate settings

chargeAppliesType.description: Indicates VC tag rate factor usage scope

vc-tag-based-rate-factor.description: Model representing VC tag based rate factor.

items.description: Identifiers of the vCenter

vcenter-to-cloud-proxy-query.description: Object used to request vCenter Mappings on a collection of collectors or collectorGroups

items.description: Collection of collectorGroups identifiers
Collection of collectors identifiers
Collection of vCenters identifiers

version.description: Represents the Product and API version information of the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations Suite

humanlyReadableReleaseDate.description: The application release date as a humanly readable string

minorMinor.description: Minor minor version of the application

releaseName.description: Name of the release that published this version

releasedDate.description: The date when was an application server with this version has been released

state.description: Represents the completion state a rebalance task.

webhook-template.description: Model that represents a webhook template. Either both of the updatedAlertRequestConfig and canceledAlertRequestConfig fields should be provided or neither of them. If those fields are not provided, the value of newAlertRequestConfig will be used while sending the notification.

wlp-history-query.description: Represents criteria of wlp history query.

endTime.description: End time for history. If not specified, the current time is used.

resourceIds.description:
Currently only one Resource Id can be provided. Support for multiple ids is not available.
The id can belong to a Datacenter, CustomDatacenter or a CloudZone for which Workload Rebalance has been performed.
If not specified, all such resources are taken into consideration.

items.description:
Currently only one Resource Id can be provided. Support for multiple ids is not available.
The id can belong to a Datacenter, CustomDatacenter or a CloudZone for which Workload Rebalance has been performed.
If not specified, all such resources are taken into consideration.

startTime.description: Start time for history. If not specified, the day prior to the endTime is used.

automationEnabled.description: Specifies whether optimization automation enabled or not

clusterExclusionEnabled.description: Turn off exclusion settings for Cluster. By default 'false'.

excludedClusterTagGroups.description: Collection of cluster excluded tag groups

excludedVmTagGroups.description: Collection of VM excluded tag groups

placementSettingsEnabled.description: Turn off placement settings. Criteria defined in 'tagGroups' will no longer be considered for VM placement. By default 'true'.

vmExclusionEnabled.description: Turn off exclusion settings for VM. By default 'false'.
